methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $280M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Quantum Machines is a Series C quantum computing company that has raised $280M to date. The broader quantum computing sector includes 11 peers across public and private markets: 3 publicly traded firms (IonQ, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum) and 8 private companies including IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Infleqtion.

A stage-progression methodology was applied, using the $280M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement. This yields an implied valuation of $476M. Peer-median analysis was not applicable given insufficient narrow peer comparables at the same stage and valuation tier within quantum computing; while 7 same-sector peers exist, none met the threshold of 3+ comparable datapoints after filtering for stage and scale proximity.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $405M to $547M, centered on $476M. This single-method triangulation carries material uncertainty. The primary limitation is the absence of tight-stage peer multiples, which would otherwise anchor the valuation. Execution risk and the nascent market structure of quantum computing introduce further variance. A more robust range would require additional data on Series C quantum computing exits or revised peer screening criteria.
